mission.title: dify: Add App permission control
mission.description: Add App permission control. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current dify deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dify version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
helm list -n dify 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dify installation.

steps.title: Review dify configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dify configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
kubectl get configmap -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dify
```
### Self Checks

### 1. Is this request related to a challenge you're experiencing? Tell me about your story.

I hope that every app can configure permissions (me or all team members), just like the permissions of the knowledge base.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add App permission control
steps.description:
# Summary

Please include a summary of the change and which issue is fixed. Please also include relevant motivation and context. List any dependencies that are required for this change.

## Support setting visibility permissions for apps

- Add options for app visibility permissions:
    - All team members
    - Some team members
    - Only me

- Fixes [Add App permission control #14086](https://github.com/langgenius/dify/issues/14086)

# Screenshots

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| ...

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
kubectl get events -n dify --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add App permission control" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
# Summary

Please include a summary of the change and which issue is fixed. Please also include relevant motivation and context. List any dependencies that are required for this change.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dify installed or the issue environment reproducible.
